creator: gerard_seibert@outlook.com
text:
(In reply to Graham Perrin from comment #197)
FreeBSD 11.4-RELEASE-p9

FreeBSD-version: 11.4-RELEASE-p13

I have spent two years attempting to update my system. FreeBSD 11.x works perfectly. At least it used to. Now, because it is EOL’s, several things are starting to break down.

I recently downloaded “FreeBSD-13.0-STABLE-amd64-20211118-3a15ccadf87-248076-memstick.img” and installed it on a USB drive and booted up the system. The following error message was then prominently and repeatedly displayed on the screen.

“uhub_reattach_port: giving up port 1 reset-device vanished: change 0xfb status 0x7fb”

The port number cycles from 1 to 4 and then the cycle restarts.

The machine works perfectly with Windows, so the problem obviously lies with FreeBSD.

Since this machine worked fine with FreeBSD prior to version 12.x, the only conclusion I can come up with is that someone fixed something that wasn’t broken. Is anyone actually working on this problem, or has it been put aside as unfixable?

Since I am not able to update several packages any longer with the older 11.x OS installed, I am assuming my only option is to investigate a Linux OS. I liked FreeBSD, but obviously, they are not interested in keeping up with current technology.
